english: rkyén (rkyen) any circumstance or event adverse to the success of an action, obstacle, hinderance, any thing opposite or hostile to the existence of another thing, mtun-rkyén (mthun rkyen), a happy, favorable circumstance, furtherance, assistance, supply, mtun-rkyén byéd-pa (mthun rkyen byed pa) c. genit. to assist in, to help to; mtun-rkyén 'dzom-po (mthun rkyen 'dzom po) altogether successful. - 3. misfortune, ill luck, calamity, rkyen zlóg-pa (rkyen zlog pa) to avert a misfortune, tégs-pa (thegs pa) to endure, túb-pa (thub pa) to brave it Mil. - cf. rgyu (rgyu).

pf. & ft. brkyaṅ (brkyang), to stretch, extend, stretch forth (one's hand to a person), put out (the tongue), spread, distend (the wings, a curtain), źabs gñyis brkyaṅ-bskúm (zhabs gnyis brkyang bskum) one leg stretched out, the other drawn in Pth.; *kyaṅ-šád-cè* (*kyang shad ce*) W. to stretch one's limbs. - brkyaṅ-šiṅ (brkyang shing) 1. 'extending-wood', an instrument of torture in Tibet, a wooden frame on which the extended arms and legs of the delinquent are fastened down, whilst burning pitch or sealing-wax is dropped on his naked breast, which procedure is called brkyaṅ-šiṅ sprád-pa (brkyang shing sprad pa), brkyaṅ-šiṅ-la bcúg-pa (brkyang shing la bcug pa) or brkyáṅ-ba (brkyang ba) 2. cross Chr. Prot. This word has been adopted on account of its etymological signification, although it differs in its form and use from the σταυρός of the N. T., which is unknown in Tibet and India. Additional explanation will be at any rate required on the part of missionaries; but much more so in the case of the kro-če (kro ce) (Ital. croce) of the Rom. Cath. missionaries of the last century. In favour of the word gsal-šiṅ (gsal shing), pointed stake for empaling a delinquent, speaks the circumstance, that this is also the original and classical signification of σταυρός, and that Buddhists from their own legends are well acquainted with the idea of martyrdom inflicted in this manner. Still gsal-šiṅ (gsal shing) leads to a conception of the death of Christ historically untrue and revolting to our feelings and is therefore better not employed; moreover it is to be assumed that in the times of the Evangelists σταυρός was the term generally used for cross, whilst in the case of gsal-šiṅ (gsal shing) no Tibetan thinks of anything else but empaling.
W., resp. *zím-kyoṅ* (*zim kyong*), *zím-tiṅ* (*zim ting*), lamp, candle, (spelling uncertain).
1. dumb, mute; k'a lkúgs-par byed-pa (kha lkugs par byed pa) to put to silence Do.; lkúgs-pa-pa (lkugs pa pa) a dumb man, -ma (-ma) woman Cs. - 2. dull, stupid Sch.

secrecy, lkóg-gi čúṅ-ma (lkog gi chung ma) Cs.: a wife kept secretly, a private concubine, lkóg-tu (lkog tu) in secrect, secretly frq.: lkóg-tu gyúr-pa (lkog tu gyur pa), lkyog-gyúr (lkyog gyur), परोक्ष, secret, hidden, out of sight Mil., Tar.; lkóg-tu gléṅ-ba (lkog tu gleng ba) to converse secretly; lk. sdód-pa (lkog sdod pa) to keep in retirement. - lkog-rṅan (lkog rngan) a reward given secretly, a bribe. - lkog-čós (lkog chos) Sch.: 'a secret doctrine'; but lkog-čós byéd-pa (lkog chos byed pa) is gen. understood: to apply one's self to religious studies or exercises in secret. - lkog jàb byéd-pa (lkog jab byed pa) to hide one's self in a lurking place: lkog jàb byed-nas lta-ba (lkog jab byed nas lta ba) to watch, to witness from a lurking-place. - lkog-zán zá-ba (lkog zan za ba) Sch. to take usury-interest in secret. - lkog-láb (lkog lab) backbiting, slander.

(vulg. *og-ma* (*'og ma*)) 1. gullet, oesophagus. - 2. wind-pipe. - 3. throat. - 4. neck. lkóg-maï lha-gòṅ (lkog ma'i lha gong) Sch., (acc. to others: lhar-gór (lhar gor)) the larynx, *koi lkóg-ma* (*kho'i lkog ma*) or *og-ma sraṅ soṅ* (*'og ma srang song*) W. his throat is swollen, he has the croup. - lkog-dkár (lkog dkar) a small nocturnal carnivorous quadruped with a white throat, marten? - lkog-gágs (lkog gags) hoarseness of voice Cs. - lkog-šál (lkog shal) dew-lap (of oxen). - lkog-sóg (lkog sog) crav., maw (of birds) Cs.
v. skad číg (skad cig), skad (skad) no. 4.
thick (of fluids, cf. slá-ba (sla ba)); ska-slád (ska slad) (Ld.: *kas-lúd* (*kas lud*)) consistence, density. - W.: *skán-te* (*skan te*).
